title: Playbook - Defender For Endpoint Vulnerability Retrieval
applies_to: Retrieve Defender for Endpoint machine vulnerability and recommendation data
prerequisites: Working Defender API token with vulnerability-management permissions
Decision on whether the output is machine-centric, software-centric, or recommendation-centric
Downstream join strategy keyed by machine ID, software ID, or CVE ID
required_permissions: Threat and Vulnerability Management read permissions appropriate to the target endpoint
If using delegated auth, ensure Defender role alignment in addition to token scopes
request_sequence: Apply OData filters for CVE, machineId, productName, severity, or vendor as needed
Optionally enrich with software detail endpoints such as GET /api/Software/{id}
Optionally pull recommendations through the recommendations endpoints
Join and normalize in your own pipeline rather than expecting one endpoint to return every dimension

confirmed_facts.fact: The vulnerabilities-by-machines API supports OData filtering on fields including cveId, machineId, productName, severity, and productVendor.
The vulnerabilities-by-machines API also supports paging controls such as $top and $skip.
Software details can be retrieved separately from software resource endpoints.

implementation_notes: Keep vulnerability extraction machine-aware from the start so later remediation reporting is easier.
Filter early by severity or product family when producing periodic reports.
Do not mix attack simulation into this playbook; that is a Graph security workflow.
common_failures: 403 from missing TVM-specific permissions even when machine inventory works
Huge result sets if no OData filtering is applied
Data duplication if you flatten machine/software rows without a clear primary key strategy
